Title: Check White Labeling widget
Steps:
1. Navigate to Admin Dashboard
2. Locate White Labeling widget
Expected Result: White Labeling widget displayed with current settings summary

Title: Set theme presets
Steps:
1. Navigate to White Labeling settings
2. Select a theme preset
3. Save
Expected Result: Theme preset applied; colors change accordingly

Title: Upload custom logo - allowed extensions
Steps:
1. Navigate to White Labeling
2. Upload logo with allowed file extension (PNG/SVG/JPG)
3. Save
Expected Result: Custom logo uploaded and displayed

Title: Upload logo - rejected for invalid extension
Steps: 1. Attempt to upload logo with non-allowed extension
Expected Result: Upload rejected with validation message

Title: Upload favicon
Steps:
1. Navigate to White Labeling
2. Upload favicon
3. Save
Expected Result: Favicon uploaded and displayed in browser tab

Title: Reset branding to defaults
Steps: 1. Click Reset to restore defaults
Expected Result: Branding restored to default theme/logo/favicon

Title: Clear all custom branding
Steps: 1. Click Clear to remove customization
Expected Result: All custom branding removed

Title: Per-organization footer links - add parent links
Steps:
1. Navigate to Organization settings
2. Add parent footer links for Organization1
3. Save
Expected Result: Parent footer links created for organization

Title: Per-organization footer links - add children links
Steps:
1. Add children footer links under parent
2. Save
Expected Result: Child footer links nested under parent

Title: Footer links and main menu links returned in GraphQL query
Preconditions: Footer and main menu links configured for organization
Steps:
1. Query white labeling settings via GraphQL with both footerLinks and mainMenuLinks fields
2. Verify response structure
3. Verify hierarchical data
Expected Result: GraphQL query returns correct footer link hierarchy and main menu links hierarchy with priority ordering

Title: Organization-specific logo and favicon
Steps:
1. Set custom logo/favicon for Organization1
2. Sign in under Organization1 account
3. Verify branding displayed
Expected Result: Organization-specific logo and favicon displayed for org users

Title: White Labeling Enabled = OFF shows default branding
Steps:
1. Set White Labeling Enabled = OFF
2. Navigate to storefront
3. Verify branding
Expected Result: Default platform branding displayed when WL disabled

Title: White Labeling Enabled = ON shows custom branding
Steps:
1. Set White Labeling Enabled = ON
2. Navigate to storefront
3. Verify branding
Expected Result: Custom logo/favicon/colors displayed when WL enabled

Title: Store-level White Labeling OFF
Steps:
1. Set store-level White Labeling = OFF
2. Verify storefront
Expected Result: Store-level WL override disables custom branding for that store

Title: Enable thumbnail job for favicons
Steps:
1. Enable thumbnail job
2. Add new run for favicon generation
3. Verify favicons generated
Expected Result: Favicon generation job runs and produces favicons in all required sizes

Title: Verify MainMenuLinkListName field exists in database
Steps:
1. Connect to QA database
2. Query WhiteLabelingSettings table schema
3. Verify MainMenuLinkListName column exists
4. Verify column type is varchar(256)
5. Verify column is nullable
Expected Result: MainMenuLinkListName column exists with correct type and nullable constraint

Title: Test GraphQL query returns mainMenuLinks field
Preconditions: Backend deployed with PR #21; Test organization configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to GraphQL playground
2. Execute white labeling settings query with mainMenuLinks field
3. Verify response contains mainMenuLinks array
Expected Result: Query executes successfully; mainMenuLinks field returned with title/url/priority; hierarchical structure supported

Title: Test mainMenuLinks with hierarchical structure
Preconditions: Link list with parent + child links created
Steps:
1. Create link list with parent (Products) and children (Laptops, Phones)
2. Set organization MainMenuLinkListName
3. Execute GraphQL query
4. Verify response structure
Expected Result: Parent link returned; Child links nested under childItems; Correct priority order

Title: Test mainMenuLinks when MainMenuLinkListName is empty
Steps:
1. Configure organization with MainMenuLinkListName = NULL
2. Execute GraphQL query
3. Verify mainMenuLinks field
Expected Result: Query executes successfully; mainMenuLinks is empty array or null; No error

Title: Test mainMenuLinks with non-existent link list name
Steps:
1. Set organization MainMenuLinkListName = non-existent-link-list
2. Execute GraphQL query
3. Verify response
Expected Result: Query executes without error; mainMenuLinks is empty; Graceful handling

Title: Test query handler retrieves correct org-specific main menu
Preconditions: Two organizations configured with different MainMenuLinkListName
Steps:
1. Create two different link lists
2. Configure Org A and Org B with different values
3. Query with Org A context
4. Query with Org B context
5. Verify results
Expected Result: Org A query returns Org A main menu; Org B query returns Org B main menu; No cross-contamination

Title: Test backward compatibility - footerLinks still works
Steps:
1. Query white labeling settings
2. Verify footerLinks field returned
3. Verify footer data correct
Expected Result: footerLinks field returned; Data matches FooterLinkListName; No breaking changes

Title: Test GraphQL query performance with mainMenuLinks
Preconditions: Organization with main menu and footer configured
Steps:
1. Execute query 10 times
2. Measure average response time
3. Compare with baseline
Expected Result: Average response time < 500ms; No significant performance degradation

Title: Verify Admin SPA displays main menu link list field
Preconditions: Admin SPA deployed; Admin user logged in
Steps:
1. Navigate to Organization White Labeling settings
2. Verify Main menu link list field exists
3. Verify field label and placeholder
Expected Result: Field displayed; Label: Main menu link list; Placeholder: Enter link list name...

Title: Configure MainMenuLinkListName via Admin SPA
Preconditions: Admin SPA accessible; Link list exists
Steps:
1. Navigate to Organization White Labeling settings
2. Enter main-menu-test in Main menu link list field
3. Save settings
4. Verify success message
Expected Result: Value saves successfully; Success message displayed; Value persisted

Title: Verify MainMenuLinkListName persisted after save
Steps:
1. Configure MainMenuLinkListName
2. Save settings
3. Refresh page
4. Navigate back to White Labeling settings
5. Verify field value
Expected Result: Field displays saved value; Value persisted; No data loss

Title: Clear MainMenuLinkListName value
Steps:
1. Navigate to White Labeling settings
2. Clear Main menu link list field
3. Save settings
4. Verify value cleared
Expected Result: Field clears successfully; Value set to NULL; Storefront falls back to default

Title: Test Admin UI localization - English
Preconditions: Admin SPA language set to English
Steps:
1. Navigate to White Labeling settings
2. Verify field label and placeholder
Expected Result: Label: Main menu link list; Placeholder: Enter link list name...

Title: Verify storefront displays per-org main menu links
Steps:
1. Sign in as organization member
2. Navigate to storefront homepage
3. Verify main menu displays
4. Verify links match configured link list
Expected Result: Main menu displays; Links match titles and URLs; Correct priority order

Title: Verify hierarchical main menu rendering (parent + children)
Steps:
1. Create link list with parent + child links
2. Configure organization
3. Sign in as organization member
4. Hover over parent menu item
5. Verify dropdown shows children
Expected Result: Parent displays; Hovering reveals dropdown; Dropdown shows child links; Links clickable

Title: Verify main menu on mobile (responsive design)
Preconditions: Organization configured; Mobile device or dev tools
Steps:
1. Open storefront on mobile
2. Sign in as organization member
3. Verify main menu displays
4. Test hamburger menu
5. Verify links accessible
Expected Result: Main menu renders correctly; Links accessible; Touch-friendly; No UI issues

Title: Verify main menu on desktop (Chrome)
Steps:
1. Open storefront in Chrome
2. Sign in as organization member
3. Verify main menu displays
4. Test hover states and dropdowns
Expected Result: Main menu renders correctly; Hover states work; Dropdowns display; No console errors

Title: Verify main menu accessibility (keyboard navigation)
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront
2. Sign in
3. Use Tab to navigate menu
4. Verify focus indicators
5. Use Enter/Space to activate
6. Use Arrow keys for dropdowns
Expected Result: All menu items keyboard-accessible; Focus indicators visible; WCAG 2.1 AA compliant

Title: Test switching between organizations updates main menu
Preconditions: Two organizations configured; User member of both
Steps:
1. Sign in as Org A member
2. Verify Org A main menu
3. Switch to Org B
4. Verify Org B main menu
5. Switch back to Org A
6. Verify Org A main menu
Expected Result: Org A: Org A menu; Org B: Org B menu; Switching updates dynamically; No cross-contamination

Title: Test main menu + footer both update on org switch
Steps:
1. Sign in as Org A member
2. Verify Org A main menu and footer
3. Switch to Org B
4. Verify Org B main menu and footer
Expected Result: Both main menu and footer update; Updates simultaneous; No inconsistent state

Title: Verify existing footer-only configs still work
Preconditions: Organization with pre-existing FooterLinkListName; MainMenuLinkListName = NULL
Steps:
1. Identify organization with footer config
2. Sign in as organization member
3. Verify footer displays
4. Verify no errors
Expected Result: Footer displays correctly; Existing configs unaffected; No migration required

Title: Verify existing logo configs still work
Steps:
1. Sign in as organization member
2. Verify logo displays in header
3. Verify logo unchanged
Expected Result: Custom logo displays correctly; No changes to logo functionality

Title: Verify existing theme preset configs still work
Steps:
1. Sign in as organization member
2. Verify theme colors and styling
3. Verify theme unchanged
Expected Result: Custom theme preset displays correctly; No changes to theme functionality

Title: Verify store-level white labeling still works
Steps:
1. Navigate to storefront without org context
2. Verify store-level branding displays
3. Verify no errors
Expected Result: Store-level white labeling unaffected; Fallback logic works

Title: Verify GraphQL query without mainMenuLinks field still works
Steps:
1. Execute white labeling query WITHOUT mainMenuLinks field
2. Verify query executes successfully
Expected Result: Query executes without errors; Backward compatibility maintained (optional field)
